How do enemy attacks work ? 
Is it from a pool that rerolls 
from a select order every time 
or completely random ?

They all have a set pattern actually even the mobs though sometimes the mobs are set up in a way where some of their attacks are delayed, like the birds in chapter 8 go debuff then big kamikaze attack, in some battles it's 3 birds, one is normal but the other two do it a turn after and their first turn is the other debuff intent where they make a card cost 3

or how the little purple things with the cloth over them all alternate between steal aliemus from highest team member and seal the highest team member, so it gets annoying when there are multiple cause they always take turns doing one or the other
That's what was confusing me, I thought it was pattern, but then sometimes they just did it out of rhythm
Thank you mate
Mhmm
Sometimes the actual battle itself has the enemy patterns changed in the order to make it a bit more challenging but if you retry that battle you see it's always the same thing in that order

There are only a few enemies who's intent actually changes  but that's usually based on what you're doing or if you meet a condition, like how Uvhash as a boss switches to a big attack once you've done a certain amount of damage to him or a few enemies where one of their intents changes between multiple kinds whenever you play a card

This feeds over into when her flails trigger bleed to do damage
Which is why she stacks it so ridiculously high

get to 2nd or 3rd chapter

but
there is somethign nice you can do
which is in the interlude section
you can try every standard character
and see their playstyle
alongside the playstyle of their realm
that should be a nice direction for you to aim

just try out all the standard characters and see who you like or which realm you like
and don't use silver for standard pulls
that's pretty much it
